decisions.rationale: Dedicated agent for plan task execution; bridge syncs memory ready-state to TaskList

decisions.decision: /team implement generates team tasks from NN-PLAN.json
decisions.rationale: Reuses existing plan structure with memoryId linkage

decisions.decision: Memory -> TaskList one-way bridge via memory.ready()
decisions.rationale: Memory is persistent source of truth; TaskList is agent-visible ephemeral layer

decisions.decision: Plan verify[] array becomes agent success criterion
decisions.rationale: Plan already defines verification; agents execute it and close on pass

decisions.decision: Enforce at plan level via files[] array
decisions.rationale: Plan tasks already specify file boundaries; no runtime enforcement needed
Simpler consistency model; memory is the durable source of truth
Implementer needs full tool access (Read, Edit, Write, Bash, Grep, Glob)

decisions.decision: Parallel within plan, sequential across plans
decisions.rationale: Plan tasks within a plan are independent; plan 02 waits for plan 01
Matches existing /implement pattern: 2 attempts then escalate

decisions.decision: Memory persists via JSONL; TaskList is ephemeral per team session

constraints: Backward compatible: /implement without teams works as before
Agent Teams is experimental — graceful degradation required
Each agent has its own context window
openQuestions: Dedicated implementer agent vs general-purpose with instructions in task description?
Should verify failures auto-create memory events or agent does it explicitly?
Mixed teams (implementer + reviewer in parallel) or separate phases?
